title: The Crusaderן¿½s Return
content:
1.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ High deeds achieved of knightly fame,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ From Palestine the champion came;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The cross upon his shoulders borne,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Battle and blast had dimmן¿½d and torn.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Each dint upon his batterן¿½d shield
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Was token of a foughten field;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And thus, beneath his ladyן¿½s bower,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He sung as fell the twilight hour:

2.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Joy to the fair! thy knight behold,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Returnן¿½d from yonder land of gold;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ No wealth he brings, nor wealth can need,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Save his good arms and battle-steed
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ His spurs, to dash against a foe,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ His lance and sword to lay him low;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Such all the trophies of his toil,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Such, and the hope of Teklaן¿½s smile!

3.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Joy to the fair! whose constant knight
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Her favour fired to feats of might;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Unnoted shall she not remain,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Where meet the bright and noble train;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Minstrel shall sing and herald tell,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Mark yonder maid of beauty well,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Tis she for whose bright eyes were won
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The listed field at Askalon!

4.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½ן¿½Note well her smile! it edged the blade
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Which fifty wives to widows made,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ When, vain his strength and Mahoundן¿½s spell,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Iconiumן¿½s turbanן¿½d Soldan fell.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Seest thou her locks, whose sunny glow
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Half shows, half shades, her neck of snow?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Twines not of them one golden thread,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But for its sake a Paynim bled.ן¿½

5.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Joy to the fair! my name unknown,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Each deed, and all its praise thine own
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Then, oh! unbar this churlish gate,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The night dew falls, the hour is late.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Inured to Syriaן¿½s glowing breath,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I feel the north breeze chill as death;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Let grateful love quell maiden shame,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And grant him bliss who brings thee fame.ן¿½
meaning:
About: Sir Walter Scott, 1st Baronet was a prolific Scottish historical novelist and poet popular throughout Europe during his time.

In some ways Scott was the first author to have a truly international career in his lifetime, with many contemporary readers all over Europe, Australia, and North America. His novels and poetry are still read, and many of his works remain classics of both English-language literature and specifically, of Scottish literature. Famous titles include Ivanhoe, Rob Roy, The Lady of the Lake, Waverley and The Heart of Midlothian.
